title: VL_INW and VL_OUTW macros require 4 arguments, but only 3 given
body:
---


Author Name: **Krzysztof Jankowski**
Original Redmine Issue: 645 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2013-05-13
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

I'm getting below errors while compiling C++ code generated by verilator v3.847:
```
error: macro "VL_INW" requires 4 arguments, but only 3 given
error: macro "VL_OUTW" requires 4 arguments, but only 3 given

```
From "verilated.h":
1. define VL_INW(name, msb,lsb, words)	WData name[words]	///< Declare input signal, 65+ bits
1. define VL_OUTW(name, msb,lsb, words)	WData name[words]	///< Declare output signal, 65+ bits

From "Vmy_top.h":
     VL_INW(arr_inp_dat,7,0);
     VL_OUTW(arr_out_dat,50,0);

Recently I recompiled v3.847 over the existing older v3.846 version, I wonder - maybe some old header files didn't get updated?
